refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process can be decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the latency of data acquisition and processing separately, then confirm the combined latency meets the p95 ≤ 1.0 s target under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry links for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency

potential_sources: Data acquisition and processing are sequential but modular functions
Interfaces between telemetry and processing modules are well-defined
coupling_check.notes: The two DPs map cleanly to the two sub-FRs with minimal coupling; changes in one DP have limited impact on the other.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry infrastructure supports required bandwidth and latency
Processing algorithms can be optimized without compromising accuracy
Risk of network congestion impacting telemetry latency
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and processing load to ensure latency targets are consistently met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry data acquisition and processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data streams from vehicles and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to vehicle control and teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE solution and external modules

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Vehicle provides raw data but does not process or update the model.
Primarily consumes updated model data; no direct role in update latency.
Focuses on safety alerts, unrelated to worksite model update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the update latency function to the NGHE Solution module centralizes responsibility and minimizes interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Dependency on network link reliability for telemetry data
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing or modularizing processing within NGHE Solution if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs directly address the refined FRs with clear mapping and minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as update latency logic is centralized in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution, supporting clear ownership and modularity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end within the NGHE Solution module using telemetry and processing metrics.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to NGHE Solution with well-defined interfaces to other modules.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link performance as it is critical to latency.
Plan for scalability within NGHE Solution to handle processing load.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and meet the latency requirement with clear ownership and verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process acquired data to update the worksite model within latency constraints

revise.why: Current level is sound; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Telemetry infrastructure supports required bandwidth and latency.
Processing algorithms can be optimized for real-time performance.
open_risks: Potential processing bottlenecks under high data load.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of telemetry data acquisition.
